1.14: The firm cannot act if the person dealing with the transaction or their immediate family is the seller.
A separate fee earner of equal standing or a separate partner must act for the lender.
1.15: The firm cannot act if the person dealing with the transaction or their immediate family is the borrower.

Acceptable List B: Employers Identity Card, Signed EU National Identity Card, Birth Certificate.

4.1: Physical property inspection is not guaranteed.
Valuation reports are electronic and cannot be forwarded.
Conveyancer must obtain a copy of the valuation from the customer.
5.1.1: Borrower must have owned property for at least 6 months for remortgages, unless inherited (written confirmation required).
Sub-sales, back-to-back transactions, and assignable/novation contracts are not acceptable.
Seller acquiring freehold/superior leasehold and immediately granting a lease to borrower is treated as a sub-sale.
Report any Unilateral Notice registered against the freehold title.
5.2.1: Assignable contracts and irrevocable powers of attorney for intervening sellers are prohibited.
5.4.4: No environmental or contaminated land reports required.
5.4.5: Firms should be members of an industry trade body abiding by the Property Ombudsman scheme with adequate insurance.
5.5.3b: Forward original or copy planning permission to the borrower.
5.5.3c: Forward original or copy building regulation consents to the borrower.
5.5.3d: Forward certificates of lawful use or development to the borrower.
5.5.4: Contact lender if Mortgage Offer does not refer to First Homes Scheme.
Ensure resale price covenants meet Affordable Housing Guidance notes.
Lender consents to the registration of the First Homes restriction without separate application.
5.7.1a: Flying freeholds are accepted subject to compliance with paragraph 5.6.2.
5.8.1: Buildings converted into max 4 flats are accepted.
If valuer did not mention title arrangements, report to the lender.
BTL properties do not require borrower occupation.
5.8.5: For two leasehold flats where cross-reversion exists: Mortgage of borrower's leasehold interest required.
Lender requires mortgage of borrower's freehold interest in the other flat, subject to its lease.
5.9.1: Commonhold is not acceptable for new mortgages.
5.13.1: Gifted deposits from immediate family (blood, spouse, child, in-law, co-habitee) allowed if not repayable.
Property must be owner-occupied, not let.
Deed of Trust or Second Charge to protect the gift is not acceptable.
Gift letter required, dated within 3 months of completion, specifying source and no interest in property.
Clear bankruptcy searches required for all contributors.
5.14.15b: Indemnity insurance for absent/insolvent landlords is accepted subject to section 9.
5.14.17a: Post-completion: Send executed Leaseholder Certificate, proof of submission to landlord, and Landlord Certificate.
5.14.17b: Report before completion if owner is a non-qualifying leaseholder.
Send Landlord Certificate or confirmation of remediation costs.
Do not complete without written instructions.
5.14.17c: Section 5.14.17 a and b do not apply to remortgages.
5.14.1: Minimum unexpired lease term: 70 years at date of mortgage.
5.14.11: Indemnity insurance for unsatisfactory lease terms is accepted subject to section 9.
5.14.12: Forward ground rent/service charge receipts to the borrower.
5.14.13: Minimum lease term: 70 years from date of mortgage.
5.15.2b: Forward management company share certificate to the borrower.
5.15.2c: Lender does not require the signed blank stock transfer form.
5.15.2d: Supply copies of memorandum and articles of association on completion; originals to borrower.
5.17.5a: Send certified copy of Power of Attorney to Bank of Scotland, Nexus, PO Box 548, Leeds, LS1 1WU only if account will be operated under it post-completion.
5.17.5b: Send certified copy of statutory declaration of non-revocation to PO Box 548, Leeds, LS1 1WU only if account will be operated under POA post-completion.
5.19.1: Shared ownership and shared equity lending are not supported.
5.20.1: Report if solar PV panel lease fails UK Finance minimum requirements via 1.11a contact.
5.20.4: No disclosure of Green Deal Plans required.
6.6.1: Distressed sales and sale/leaseback arrangements are not acceptable.
6.6.3: Forward counterpart or certified copy tenancy agreements to the borrower.
6.6.4: HMOs accepted with maximum 5 unrelated occupiers on a single AST.
Mandatory HMO licence must be in place.
6.7.1: Accepted warranties: NHBC, Premier Guarantee, LABC, Buildzone, Checkmate, ABC+, Advantage HCI, Aedis, Ark, BLP, Cadis, CIR, Compariqo, FMB, Global, ICW, One Guarantee, Protek, Q Assure, Thomas Miller.
Only required for new properties occupied for the first time.
6.7.2: Forward warranty documentation to the borrower.
6.7.3: Forward assignments of building standards indemnity schemes to the borrower.
6.7.4: Properties without warranty accepted if site has max 15 properties and construction was monitored by a qualified consultant.
6.7.6: Professional consultant's certificate is not required to be sent.
6.8.1: No retention required for unadopted roads if managed by residents or management company.
6.10.2: Funds released to Nominated Solicitor or Borrower.
7.3: Consent required for occupants 17+ except borrower's children or BTL tenants on AST.
7.4: Report if mortgage is sole name but borrower is married/co-habiting; obtain consent.
8.1: Conveyancer may not advise specified third parties.
9.1: Forward indemnity insurance policy to the borrower.
9.2: Minimum indemnity limit: Mortgage advance plus 10%.
10.2b: Urgent requests incur a £20 CHAPS fee deducted from advance.
10.3: Deductions include retentions and sums specified in the Mortgage Offer.
10.7: Return delayed funds electronically without deductions.
Use codes: RAD (Returned Advance), FADV (Returned further advance), DUP (Duplicate).
10.10: Advance can be held for 5 working days.
Interest charged if not returned by close of business on 4th working day after scheduled completion.
10.11: Interest charged daily at mortgage offer rate from original completion date.
